verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent complex (LAP-TGFβ1) that is covalently/non-covalently tethered to cell-surface TM partners — notably GARP (LRRC32) on Tregs and platelets, and LRRC33 on myeloid cells — via disulfide bonds, holding latent TGFβ1 stably on the outer leaflet. This is wash-resistant covalent tethering to a TM scaffold, the basis for clinical anti-GARP/TGFβ1 and anti-LRRC33/TGFβ1 antibody programs (e.g., livmoniplimab, SRK-181) that engage the surface-anchored latent complex. Mature TGFβ1 also exists as a soluble cytokine, but the surface-tethered latent form is a bona fide cell-surface entity.
